context: Faculty members shall submit grade reports within the specified period after final examination. Three days are allowed for grading papers per section. context: Scholarship for children/legal dependents of NEUST personnel: beneficiaries are spouses/children of NEUST personnel holding regular appointment on permanent status. context: Student Judicial Council (SJC) composed of five bona fide students appointed by University President upon recommendation of USG through Dean of Student Affairs. Student Judicial Divisions (SJD) may be created for campuses with same composition. Functions: receive complaints from student against another student, conduct preliminary proceedings, initiate conciliatory proceedings. Jurisdiction: SJC hears complaints involving students from different academic units or against USG officers; SJD hears complaints involving students from same unit. Term of one year. Complaint procedure: file six copies of letter to SJC/SJD chairman, notice to respondent with 72 hours to answer, complainant has three days to reply, fact-finding within 10 days, conciliation attempted, if fails, elevate to Student Discipline Board within 10 days.

context: USG General Provisions: The seal of the USG. The clasped hands represent the unity of the students and the university. The eye represents the University Student Government promoting the general welfare of the students, symbolized by the figures. The wheel represents the wheel of progress and institutional objectives. The color shall be blue, red and yellow. The Assembly may, by law, adopt a new name, seal, hymn, march, or new uniform for the entire organization, which shall be truly reflective and symbolic of the ideas, history, and traditions of the students of the university. context: USG Legislative Branch: legislative power vested in University Student Assembly. Assembly composed of members determined by 1:300 pro rata basis per College/Campus Council and one representative from each sector: Women, Non-Political Organizations, Cultural Groups, Campus Publications, ROTC. context: The Student Loan Assistance Program (Appendix C) legal bases include the 1987 Philippine Constitution Article XIV Section 1,2,3 which mandates the State to promote quality education and establish scholarship grants and student loan programs especially for the underprivileged. context: Suggested disciplinary sanctions for stealing: First Commission - Restoration of the property or equivalent amount, reprimand and written apology jointly signed by the parents or guardian. Second Commission - Restoration, fine twice the amount of the stolen property, and one semester suspension. Third Commission - Restoration, fine three times the amount, and from one year suspension to dismissal. For dishonesty/intellectual dishonesty: First Commission - letter of apology and reprimand plus one month to one semester suspension. Second Commission - one semester to one-year suspension. Third Commission - one-year suspension to dismissal from the University.

qas.question: What happens on third commission of stealing?

answers.text: Restoration, fine three times the amount, and from one year suspension to dismissal

context: Student Discipline Board composition: five members appointed by University President with one representative each from Administration, Faculty, Student Body. context: Student Loan registration loan details: Maximum loanable amount P3,000. Interest payable four percent per semester. context: Prompt and regular attendance in all classes is required from the first meeting. A student is considered absent if not present within the first third fraction of the scheduled class time. context: Grading system: 'INC' indicates requirements not accomplished; given to students with passing grades but missing requirements. Must be completed within one year, otherwise becomes '5'. 'UD' means Unofficially Dropped - student did not submit dropping form. 'Dropped' indicates formal dropping. Faculty submit grades within specified period; three days allowed per section. If multiple sections, submit one report every three-day period; all reports within five days after last exam day. No student shall ask for grade recommendation; violation loses credit. Changes of grades for erroneous entry can increase but never decrease. Grade revision committee created by Dean if grade resulted from arbitrary decision. Request within thirty days after receipt of final grade.

context: NEUST Student Election Code: This code governs all student elections. Election Committee constituted in each department/college/campus. Board of Election Inspectors formed for each student council. Board of Canvassers takes charge of counting election returns. context: Warning: Any student who obtained a final grade below '3' in subjects equivalent to 5 academic units at the end of the semester shall be warned by the Dean. context: All matters pertaining to student conduct and discipline are governed by Part V of the Handbook. Teachers and professors are deemed persons in authority and should be accorded due respect and protection under Art. 153 (3) of the Revised Penal Code as amended by PD 299 and the Education Act of 1982. context: Student Judicial Council (SJC) composed of five bona fide students appointed by University President upon recommendation of USG through Dean of Student Affairs. Student Judicial Divisions (SJD) may be created. Jurisdiction: SJC hears complaints involving students from different academic units or against USG officers; SJD hears complaints involving students from same unit. Complaint procedure: file six copies of letter to chairman, notice to respondent with 72 hours to answer, complainant has three days to reply, fact-finding within 10 days. If conciliation fails, elevate to Student Discipline Board within 10 days. The Student Discipline Board has chairman, arbiter (lawyer), secretary. Arbiter and secretary have no vote.

context: Program continuity for Student Loan Assistance Program: the interest of the loan release shall be utilized for the maintenance and operations of the Program. The Loan fund and interest income shall be treated as trust fund. The interest income shall be expended for direct and necessary expense needed in the operations of the program. At least thirty percent (30%) of the interest income shall be ploughed back to the capital build up of the loan fund. context: Cheating in examination and/or quizzes: First Commission - Decrease of grade from the lowest allowable to automatic grade of '5'. Second Commission - Automatic grade of '5' or suspension for one semester. Third Commission - Automatic grade of '5' in the subject and suspension of one-year or dismissal from the college. Oppression: Threatening another with infliction upon his person, honor or property - First Commission: two weeks to one semester suspension; Second: one semester to one-year; Third: one-year to dismissal. Threat against the life of another shall be punished with expulsion even in first commission. Fighting resulting in death shall be punished with expulsion even in first commission.
